name: Danny Casolaro Death
description: Danny Casolaro was a freelance journalist who died in Martinsburg, West Virginia, in August 1991. His death was officially ruled a suicide by slashing his wrists. However, numerous conspiracy theories surround his death, fueled by his investigation into what he called "The Octopus," a purported global network involved in illegal activities. Proponents of these theories believe Casolaro was murdered to prevent him from exposing this network. They point to suspicious circumstances surrounding his death, including the lack of blood at the scene inconsistent with severe wrist lacerations, missing notes and documents, and alleged threats he received before his death. The "Octopus" theory implicates various entities, including intelligence agencies, government officials, and organized crime figures, in a web of conspiracies involving arms dealing, drug trafficking, and stolen software. Mainstream perspectives, supported by the official investigation, maintain that Casolaro was deeply troubled, suffering from depression and financial difficulties, and that his death was a suicide. Critics of the conspiracy theories argue that the evidence is circumstantial and that alternative explanations for the suspicious circumstances exist, such as sloppy police work or the journalist's own personal struggles. The case continues to be a subject of debate and speculation, often cited in discussions of government corruption and the potential dangers of investigative journalism.
subtitle: Journalist's death shrouded in conspiracy.

claims.claim: Casolaro was murdered to silence him.
claims.evidence: Missing documents, alleged threats, inconsistencies at the crime scene.
claims.criticism: Official ruling of suicide, lack of conclusive evidence of foul play, possible misinterpretation of evidence.

claims.claim: "The Octopus" network exists and was responsible for Casolaro's death.
claims.evidence: Casolaro's research notes, alleged connections between various scandals.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete evidence of a single, cohesive "Octopus" organization, disparate scandals linked together speculatively.

claims.claim: Government agencies were involved in Casolaro's death.
claims.evidence: Alleged connections between individuals implicated in "The Octopus" and government agencies.
claims.criticism: Circumstantial connections, lack of direct evidence linking government agencies to the murder.

keyFigures.name: Christian Hansen
keyFigures.role: Investigative journalist, investigated the case and wrote a book arguing against suicide

keyFigures.name: Rodney Stich
keyFigures.role: Author and self-proclaimed whistleblower; promoted conspiracy theories involving Casolaro

keyFigures.name: M. Wesley Swearingen
keyFigures.role: Former FBI Agent who investigated and argued the death was suspicious

timeline.event: Danny Casolaro's death in Martinsburg, West Virginia.
Emergence and proliferation of conspiracy theories surrounding his death.

sources.author: Kenn Thomas and Jim Keith
sources.work: The Octopus: Secret Government and the Killing of Danny Casolaro
sources.title: Kenn Thomas and Jim Keith

sources.author: Cheri Seymour
sources.work: American Predator: The Untold Story of Carlos Lehder and the Medellin Cartel
sources.title: Cheri Seymour
